summary: Tested billing module + new marketing landing/pricing/billing pages plus regression. Backend: 21/24 pass, 1 skip (no emails), 2 fail (1 known stale-state in applications test, 1 real 500 in /billing/status/{session_id}). Frontend: all 9 critical flows PASS (Landing hero+features, /pricing 3 cards, logged-out OAuth redirect, logged-in /->dashboard, sidebar Billing nav, /billing card, /pricing logged-in checkout call returns Stripe URL and browser actually lands on Stripe checkout US$19.00 page, /billing/return shows Verifying state).

critical.issue: Returns 500 when polling a real Stripe-created session. Root cause: emergentintegrations.payments.stripe.checkout.get_checkout_status fails pydantic validation — 'CheckoutStatusResponse.metadata: Input should be a valid dictionary [type=dict_type, input_value=<StripeObject ...>]'. Stripe returns metadata as a StripeObject, not a plain dict, and the library's CheckoutStatusResponse model rejects it. This blocks the entire success/return flow — users will never see Pro/Team activated client-side via polling (webhook fallback still works). Reproduce: POST /api/billing/checkout (returns valid session), then GET /api/billing/status/<that_session_id>.

minor.issue: test_create_and_update_application failed because prior runs already advanced the job's application to 'interview'. Endpoint behavior is correct (it returns existing app rather than creating duplicate); test needs unique job or cleanup.

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix /api/billing/status/{session_id} 500. Wrap stripe.get_checkout_status() in try/except CheckoutError, OR upgrade/patch emergentintegrations so CheckoutStatusResponse accepts StripeObject metadata (cast to dict via dict(metadata) before pydantic validation). Until fixed, users completing a Stripe checkout will see indefinite 'Verifying' on /billing/return and only get plan activation via the webhook path.
Make application creation test idempotent (use a fresh job_id each run or delete the application after) — non-blocking.
critical_code_review_comments: routes_billing.py is clean and well-structured: PLANS defined server-side (good security), idempotent activation guard (already_paid check), webhook duplicates plan activation correctly.
BillingReturn.jsx caps polling at 8 attempts × 2s = 16s — reasonable, but combined with the /status 500 bug, users will hit the timeout state every time even on successful payments.
Pricing.jsx upgrade() catches errors but only resets busy state — no user-visible error toast. If checkout fails, user just sees button reset with no feedback.
Stripe webhook endpoint correctly returns 400 on invalid signature, accepts host_url from request.base_url (proper for proxy).
test_credentials: Bearer test_session_career_os / cookie session_token=test_session_career_os (user_testseed01). NOTE: session had been deleted from DB; I re-seeded users+sessions collections before tests.
seed_data_creation: Re-seeded user_testseed01 in users collection + test_session_career_os in sessions collection (was missing — likely DB reset between iterations). Also POST /api/seed-me via autouse fixture.
context_for_next_testing_agent: If auth tests fail with 401, re-seed users+sessions collections (snippet in /app/memory/test_credentials.md). The /api/billing/status 500 is a third-party library issue in emergentintegrations — main agent should either pin/patch the lib or wrap the call. Frontend flows are fully working including real Stripe redirect (US$19 Pro / US$49 Team verified on Stripe sandbox UI).
rca_of_the_issue: POST /api/billing/checkout works (creates real Stripe sandbox session, persists payment_transactions doc with status=initiated). GET /api/billing/status/{session_id} crashes inside emergentintegrations because Stripe SDK returns session.metadata as a StripeObject (dict-like proxy) and emergentintegrations.payments.stripe.checkout.CheckoutStatusResponse declares metadata: dict — pydantic v2 rejects the StripeObject. Fix: in routes_billing.check_status, wrap the get_checkout_status call: try/except CheckoutError; OR monkey-patch by reading the session directly via stripe SDK and constructing the response manually; OR update emergentintegrations to coerce metadata via dict(metadata) before pydantic validation. The webhook path is unaffected because handle_webhook uses different parsing.
